reasoning: The resolution criteria require two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth with the starting quarter between start of 2024 and end of 2025. 2024 saw positive GDP growth all year. 2025 Q1 was negative (-0.5%), but Q2 and Q3 rebounded strongly (+3.8% and +4.3% respectively). Therefore, the only remaining opportunity for this to resolve YES is if both Q4 2025 and Q1 2026 are negative. However, as of late December 2025, the Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model estimates Q4 2025 growth at a robust +3.0%. The chances of Q4 2025 collapsing into negative territory despite real-time indicators pointing to ~3% growth, alongside a subsequent negative Q1 2026, are vanishingly small. The Manifold market's 1% estimate reflects this near-impossibility. I assign the lowest allowable probability (2%).
